name: lint-debt-experiment
description: Example autoresearch-style experiment: iteratively edit ONE file, keeping only edits that lower a numeric metric (here: lint warning count). Configure the metric and target in harness.config.json.
fixPrompt: Lower the metric by fixing real issues in the target file (e.g. add explicit types, guard unsafe stringification, reorder imports). Fix the underlying issue — the metric measures the whole project, so only genuine fixes in the target move it. Make one focused, reviewable change per iteration.
guardrails: Edit ONLY the declared target file — the runner reverts the target on regression but does NOT revert edits elsewhere.
Never add suppression comments (type-checker ignores, lint-disable) to lower the count without fixing the cause — that is experiment fraud.
Never weaken linter/compiler configuration to drop warnings.
Preserve runtime behavior — a type-only or formatting fix must not change what the code does.
onExhausted: Stop. Report the baseline and best metric values, which edits were kept vs reverted, and whether the remaining issues in the target need a config decision or a larger refactor than one file allows.
